# Command-Line Help for `jj-gh`

This document contains the help content for the `jj-gh` command-line program.

## `jj-gh`

Opinionated `jj` tools for working with GitHub from your terminal

**Usage:** `jj-gh [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>`

###### **Subcommands:**

- `pr` ‚Äî Commands to work with PRs
- `debug` ‚Äî Diagnostic subcommands. Useful for inspecting the resolved config and pre-flight checks
- `completions` ‚Äî Generate completions (on stdout) for the specified shell

###### **Options:**

- `-v`, `--verbose` ‚Äî Increase log verbosity (repeat for more, e.g. `-vv`)
- `-q`, `--quiet` ‚Äî Drop log level to `ERROR`
- `--log-level <LEVEL>` ‚Äî Set log level explicitly, overrides `-v` and `-q`

## `jj-gh pr`

Commands to work with PRs

**Usage:** `jj-gh pr <COMMAND>`

###### **Subcommands:**

- `create` ‚Äî Open your preferred editor to create a PR from a revision. Opens your editor to a markdown file where you can write the PR description, and set PR metadata like title, labels, auto-merge, etc. via the markdown frontmatter. This supports stacked PRs; by default the base branch is set to the closest ancestor bookmark if one exists, otherwise `trunk()`
- `fetch` ‚Äî Fetch a pull request into a local bookmark. This command accepts either a revision ID or a PR number. If given a revision ID, the PR number will be looked up via the API. Requires a colocated git repository; the special `refs/pull/123/head` ref is fetched via `git` because `jj` cannot yet fetch arbitrary refs
- `auto-merge` ‚Äî Enable auto-merge on a PR. Accepts either a PR number or a revision; with a revision, the PR is looked up by the rev's local bookmark. Fails if the repo does not allow auto-merge
- `log` ‚Äî Like `jj log`, but injects PR metadata (number, CI status, URL) as template aliases keyed by `commit_id` and renders inline PR info in the default template. Any arguments after `--` are forwarded to the underlying `jj log` invocation, e.g. `jj-gh pr log -- -r 'mine()'`

## `jj-gh pr create`

Open your preferred editor to create a PR from a revision. Opens your editor to a markdown file where you can write the PR description, and set PR metadata like title, labels, auto-merge, etc. via the markdown frontmatter. This supports stacked PRs; by default the base branch is set to the closest ancestor bookmark if one exists, otherwise `trunk()`

**Usage:** `jj-gh pr create [OPTIONS] <REV>`

**Command Alias:** `c`

###### **Arguments:**

- `<REV>` ‚Äî Revision to create the PR from

###### **Options:**

- `--base <BRANCH>` ‚Äî Override the base bookmark. Default: closest ancestor bookmark on the stack, falling back to the remote's `main` / `master` / configured `default_base_branch`
- `--draft <DRAFT>` ‚Äî Force the PR to be a draft. Overrides config (default: `draft = false`). Use `--no-draft` to force non-draft
- `--no-draft` ‚Äî Force the PR to be non-draft. Overrides config
- `--auto-merge <AUTO_MERGE>` ‚Äî Enable auto-merge on the PR after creation (merges once required checks pass). Overrides config (default: `auto_merge = false`). Use `--no-auto-merge` to force no auto-merge
- `--no-auto-merge` ‚Äî Disable auto-merge on the created PR. Overrides config
- `--auto-merge-method <METHOD>` ‚Äî Merge method used when auto-merge is enabled. Overrides config `auto_merge_method` (default `merge`)

  Possible values: `merge`, `squash`, `rebase`

- `--template <PATH_OR_NAME>` ‚Äî Template path or name under `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/`. Default: `template_path` in config, else auto-detect `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`. CLI path resolution needs the repo root, so this stays out of figment merging and is handled by `resolve_template_path` at handler time
- `--no-template` ‚Äî Skip template selection entirely
- `-e`, `--editor <CMD>` ‚Äî Editor command; shell-words split, e.g. `--editor "nvim +7"`. Default: `editor` in config, then `$VISUAL`, then `$EDITOR`
- `--gh-askpass <CMD>` ‚Äî Askpass helper command that prints a GitHub token on stdout; shell-words split, e.g. `--gh-askpass "op read op://Vault/gh/token"`. Default: `gh_askpass` in config, then `$GH_ASKPASS`
- `--askpass-timeout <SECS>` ‚Äî Timeout in seconds for the askpass helper. Default: 20

## `jj-gh pr fetch`

Fetch a pull request into a local bookmark. This command accepts either a revision ID or a PR number. If given a revision ID, the PR number will be looked up via the API. Requires a colocated git repository; the special `refs/pull/123/head` ref is fetched via `git` because `jj` cannot yet fetch arbitrary refs.

See: <https://github.com/jj-vcs/jj/issues/4388>

**Usage:** `jj-gh pr fetch [OPTIONS] <PR_NUM>`

**Command Alias:** `f`

###### **Arguments:**

- `<PR_NUM>` ‚Äî PR number to fetch

###### **Options:**

- `-t`, `--template <STR>` ‚Äî Override the bookmark template. Default: `pr_fetch_bookmark_template` in config, else `pr-{number}/{branch}`. Placeholders: `{number}`, `{branch}` (head.ref), `{user}` (head.user.login), `{repo}` (head.repo.name). `{{` / `}}` are literal braces
- `-f`, `--force` ‚Äî Replace an existing local bookmark of the same name
- `--gh-askpass <CMD>` ‚Äî Askpass helper command that prints a GitHub token on stdout; shell-words split, e.g. `--gh-askpass "op read op://Vault/gh/token"`. Default: `gh_askpass` in config, then `$GH_ASKPASS`
- `--askpass-timeout <SECS>` ‚Äî Timeout in seconds for the askpass helper. Default: 20

## `jj-gh pr auto-merge`

Enable auto-merge on a PR. Accepts either a PR number or a revision; with a revision, the PR is looked up by the rev's local bookmark. Fails if the repo does not allow auto-merge

**Usage:** `jj-gh pr auto-merge [OPTIONS] <PR_NUM|REV>`

**Command Alias:** `am`

###### **Arguments:**

- `<PR_NUM|REV>` ‚Äî PR number, or revision ID to look up a PR from

###### **Options:**

- `-m`, `--method <METHOD>` ‚Äî Merge method used when enabling auto-merge. Overrides config `auto_merge_method` (default `merge`)

  Possible values: `merge`, `squash`, `rebase`

- `--gh-askpass <CMD>` ‚Äî Askpass helper command that prints a GitHub token on stdout; shell-words split, e.g. `--gh-askpass "op read op://Vault/gh/token"`. Default: `gh_askpass` in config, then `$GH_ASKPASS`
- `--askpass-timeout <SECS>` ‚Äî Timeout in seconds for the askpass helper. Default: 20

## `jj-gh pr log`

Like `jj log`, but injects PR metadata (number, CI status, URL) as template aliases keyed by `commit_id` and renders inline PR info in the default template. Any arguments after `--` are forwarded to the underlying `jj log` invocation, e.g. `jj-gh pr log -- -r 'mine()'`.

The following template aliases are available for use if you pass your own template instead of using the default:

`pr_number`, `pr_url`, `pr_ci_status`, `pr_merge_status`, `pr_meta` (formatted string containing all PR information).

**Usage:** `jj-gh pr log [OPTIONS] [-- <JJ_LOG_ARGS>...]`

**Command Alias:** `l`

###### **Arguments:**

- `<JJ_LOG_ARGS>` ‚Äî Arguments forwarded verbatim to the underlying `jj log` invocation. Pass after `--`, e.g. `jj-gh pr log -- -r 'mine()' -T builtin_log_compact`. If you pass `-T` / `--template`, the default PR-aware template is not applied; use the injected aliases (`pr_number`, `pr_url`, `pr_ci_status`, `pr_meta`) from your own template. `pr_meta` is the pre-formatted hyperlinked PR number + colored CI icon (empty for commits without a PR)

###### **Options:**

- `--gh-askpass <CMD>` ‚Äî Askpass helper command that prints a GitHub token on stdout; shell-words split, e.g. `--gh-askpass "op read op://Vault/gh/token"`. Default: `gh_askpass` in config, then `$GH_ASKPASS`
- `--askpass-timeout <SECS>` ‚Äî Timeout in seconds for the askpass helper. Default: 20
- `--nerdfonts <NERDFONTS>` ‚Äî Force enable the use of nerdfont icons in the default `pr log` template. Overrides config. Use `--no-nerdfonts` to disable
- `--no-nerdfonts` ‚Äî Force the default `pr log` template not to use nerdfont icons. Overrides config

## `jj-gh debug`

Diagnostic subcommands. Useful for inspecting the resolved config and pre-flight checks

**Usage:** `jj-gh debug <COMMAND>`

###### **Subcommands:**

- `config` ‚Äî Print the merged config with the token rendered as `***`
- `auth` ‚Äî Resolve the GitHub token and report success or failure. Never prints the token itself
- `rev` ‚Äî Resolve a revision and print commit info, ancestor bookmark, remote URLs, and the detected default branch
- `pr-lookup` ‚Äî Pre-flight lookup for a PR: resolve the target, check if a PR is already open for the head, and confirm the base branch exists on the remote

## `jj-gh debug config`

Print the merged config with the token rendered as `***`

**Usage:** `jj-gh debug config`

## `jj-gh debug auth`

Resolve the GitHub token and report success or failure. Never prints the token itself

**Usage:** `jj-gh debug auth`

## `jj-gh debug rev`

Resolve a revision and print commit info, ancestor bookmark, remote URLs, and the detected default branch

**Usage:** `jj-gh debug rev <REV>`

###### **Arguments:**

- `<REV>`

## `jj-gh debug pr-lookup`

Pre-flight lookup for a PR: resolve the target, check if a PR is already open for the head, and confirm the base branch exists on the remote

**Usage:** `jj-gh debug pr-lookup <REV>`

###### **Arguments:**

- `<REV>`

## `jj-gh completions`

Generate completions (on stdout) for the specified shell.

Without flags, emits a standalone completion script for the `jj-gh` binary. With `--jj-alias <NAME> --subcommand <NAME>` (both required together), emits an overlay that adds completions for `jj <jj-alias> <tab>` on top of jj's own completion script (source the overlay _after_ `jj util completion <shell>`).

**Usage:** `jj-gh completions [OPTIONS] <SHELL>`

###### **Arguments:**

- `<SHELL>`

  Possible values: `bash`, `elvish`, `fish`, `powershell`, `zsh`

###### **Options:**

- `--jj-alias <NAME>` ‚Äî Emit an overlay for `jj <NAME> <tab>` instead of the standalone `jj-gh` script. Pass the jj alias name (e.g. `pr`). Must be paired with `--subcommand`
- `--subcommand <NAME>` ‚Äî jj-gh top-level subcommand whose tree the overlay describes (e.g. `pr`). Must be paired with `--jj-alias`

  Possible values: `pr`
